The rain battered the windows of the farmhouse while Martha stared at the folded piece of cream-colored paper lying on the desk. For weeks, the silence between them had grown heavy, filled only by the distant crackle of artillery miles away. Her husband, Elias, was still fighting behind the front lines, and all she had left to tether their lives together was the next **letter** he managed to send.

Martha picked up her pen and smoothed the paper flat. She hesitated, staring at the blank sheet, knowing that distance stretched time until nothing seemed real. She whispered a promise aloud, deciding she would **letter** him every morning until the war ended. She wanted to flood the trenches with his name, inscribing it on everything that remained in this quiet house. She dipped her nib into the dark ink, prepared to begin the work of saying the things the world could not hear.<|im_end|>
